headline: The common direct-login/auth dispatcher at 0x140154470 materializes route and credential state on the auth controller, then writes a computed integer/hash value into a settings-style string map through 0x14067A3C0. This is still pre-HTTP/pre-packet state; it does not construct the final auth endpoint or request body directly.

argument_layout.meaning: refcounted pair copied from [R8] and [R8+0x8]; old controller+0x28 object is released through vtable slots +0x8/+0x10 when its refcounts drop
if arg10 is nonzero, arg11 is copied; otherwise +0x120 is forced to -1

hash_and_map_handoff: If controller+0x11C is not -1, that value is copied to controller+0xC0 and used as the map value.
Otherwise, if controller+0x120 is not -1, controller+0x120 is copied to controller+0xC0 and used as the map value.
Otherwise, the dispatcher computes a 31x accumulator over controller+0x70, stores it at controller+0xC0, and only continues when the controller+0x70 string length is nonzero.
The final handoff is CALL 0x14067A3C0 at 0x140154692 with RCX=controller+0xB70, RDX=[controller+0xC38], and R8=the selected/computed controller+0xC0 value.

entry_writer_0x14067A3C0.previously_mapped_usage: acceptedDownloads bootstrap state uses this same function with RDX='acceptedDownloads' and R8D=1.
behavior: Converts the signed integer in R8 into a decimal string buffer.
Treats RDX as a C-string key and copies it into a local small-string object.
Computes an FNV-1a style hash over the key string using 0x811C9DC5 and 0x1000193.
Uses RCX as a map/table object with buckets at +0x58 and bucket count at +0x60.
Walks an existing bucket chain through node+0x30 and compares keys with 0x1400B1D70.
If an entry exists, updates the node value at node+0x18 with the decimal string.
If no entry exists, calls 0x14067AFF0 against map+0x50 to allocate/insert a new node, then writes the decimal string into the new node+0x18.
Calls 0x140679D20 after mutation, consistent with a post-update/rebalance/finalize step for this settings-map family.
negative_findings: No endpoint literals or content-type literals are referenced from 0x14067A3C0.
The late indirect CALL R10 at 0x14067A784 is gated by the local small-string ownership bit and appears to be cleanup/deallocation through a global service/allocator table, not the auth transport.

caller_family_0x140154470.notes: Gated by controller+0x10, client/root+0x19838, client/root+0x19F60 == 0x1E, and controller+0xEC; consumes client/root+0x19878.
First of three local route-1 materialization calls in the same function.
Route-1 materialization with stack arg9 set to 1, causing controller+0x100 to be marked.
Route-1 materialization in the same multi-branch helper; resets/uses controller+0x11C/+0x120 around the call.
Cached username/password direct-login preparation path from 0x140026E80.
Route-1 helper consuming client/root+0x19878 and controller state.
Alternate/direct-login route helper with stack arg9 set to 1, causing controller+0x100 to be marked.
Route-1 helper using controller+0x70 as one string/range argument.
Route-1 helper with stack arg9 set to 1 and stack arg10 sourced from a byte flag.
SSO direct-login continuation path reached from 0x1401536B0 through 0x1401541E0.
SSO/direct-login continuation path using controller+0x70 and forcing stack arg11 to -1.
External caller that first loads the auth controller from client/root+0x198D0, then applies the same route-1 materialization.

opennxt_implications: The route selector at controller+0x20 is concrete input into later login/session behavior; selector 2 now ties to cached credentials and SSO/direct-login paths, while selector 1 covers primary/preparatory auth-controller paths.
For server compatibility work, failures immediately after direct-login setup may happen before any endpoint is assembled: this layer is still preparing controller state and map entries.

controller+0xB70 is very likely a settings-map/table object embedded in the auth controller; controller+0xC38 is the key string used for the map entry, and controller+0xC0 is the integer value written under that key.
The map writer is shared with init/acceptedDownloads, so OpenNXT-side diagnostics should treat it as generic client state publication rather than an auth-only request builder.
next_targets: Trace consumers of controller+0x20, controller+0x100, controller+0xC0, and the controller+0xB70 map to locate the later login packet/request builder.
Resolve the controller+0xC38 key producer so the exact auth map key written by direct-login setup is named.
Map 0x1401531E9 and 0x140153C5F more deeply to name the selector-1 and selector-2 helper variants beyond SSO/cached direct-login.
Follow 0x140679D20 in the settings-map family to determine whether it finalizes, rehashes, publishes, or notifies after map mutation.
